---
title: "Labs"
subtitle: "The four UCF research groups that comprise BioMIND"
description: "BioMIND is an umbrella research cluster connecting CASL, Human-CAIR, MoML, and AI-AIR at the University of Central Florida."
---

BioMIND is an **umbrella research cluster** connecting complementary UCF laboratories working across **BioAI**, **molecular intelligence**, **complex adaptive systems**, **trustworthy AI**, **human-centered AI**, and **applied machine learning**.

## Member labs

::: {.bm-grid .bm-grid-2}

::: {.bm-lab-card}
### CASL — Complex Adaptive Systems Laboratory
[Director — Ivan I. Garibay]{.bm-lab-pi}

Artificial intelligence, complex adaptive systems, network science, agent-based modeling, information diffusion, misinformation dynamics, cognitive cybersecurity, foundation models, and large language models.

[Visit CASL →](casl.qmd)
:::

::: {.bm-lab-card}
### Human-CAIR — Human-Centered AI Research Lab
[Director — Ozlem Ozmen Garibay]{.bm-lab-pi}

Human-centered, responsible, and explainable AI; fairness and bias mitigation; human-machine teams; social cybersecurity; and trustworthy BioAI.

[Visit Human-CAIR →](human-cair.qmd)
:::

::: {.bm-lab-card}
### MoML — Molecular Machine Learning
[Lead — Ozlem Ozmen Garibay]{.bm-lab-pi}

Molecular machine learning, interpretable models for drug discovery, drug-target interaction prediction, molecular generation, diffusion models, and AI-assisted biological discovery.

[Visit MoML →](moml.qmd)
:::

::: {.bm-lab-card}
### AI-AIR — Applied Innovative AI Research Lab
[Director — Niloofar Yousefi]{.bm-lab-pi}

AI/ML, drug discovery, bioinformatics, computational social science, generative AI for science, AI-guided nanomedicine, and applied AI.

[Visit AI-AIR →](aiair.qmd)
:::

:::

## How the cluster works

::: {.bm-grid .bm-grid-2}

::: {.bm-card}
### Shared themes, distinct identities
Each lab retains its own scientific identity and history. BioMIND provides a unified public face, shared seminars, and joint initiatives across labs.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Co-advised students
Students with cross-cutting interests can be co-advised by faculty across the cluster, enabling research that bridges BioAI, trustworthy AI, and complex systems.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Joint projects and proposals
Faculty across BioMIND pursue joint grant proposals and collaborative research projects, particularly at the intersection of BioAI and responsible AI.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### External engagement
A common cluster presence simplifies engagement with funders, industry partners, government, and prospective students.
:::

:::
